rubric.task_summary: Extract structured meeting data into JSON with specific date, attendees, and action items.

assumptions: The date 'Thursday March 12 2026' refers to the date of the meeting.
The phrase 'end of this week' for Marcus's task refers to the end of the week containing March 12, 2026, which is explicitly clarified as March 13 in the text.
The attendees listed after 'Present:' are the only attendees.
known_risks: Ambiguity in 'end of this week' could theoretically refer to the end of the calendar week (Sunday March 15), but the text explicitly states 'March 13', resolving this ambiguity.

assumptions: The meeting date is Thursday, March 12, 2026, as stated in the note.
The attendees listed are Alex Chen, Priya Raman, and Marcus Weiss.
Action items are explicitly assigned to Alex, Priya, and Marcus with specific due dates.
End of this week for Marcus refers to March 13, 2026, based on the meeting date of March 12, 2026.
known_risks: Misinterpreting 'end of this week' if the week boundary is different, but the note explicitly says March 13.

candidates.solution: {"date": "2026-03-12", "attendees": ["Alex Chen", "Priya Raman", "Marcus Weiss"], "action_items": [{"owner": "Alex Chen", "task": "finalize the roadmap draft", "due_date": "2026-03-19"}, {"owner": "Priya Raman", "task": "customer-research writeup", "due_date": "2026-03-25"}, {"owner": "Marcus Weiss", "task": "follows up with legal on the DPA", "due_date": "2026-03-13"}]}
assumptions: The meeting date is explicitly stated as March 12, 2026.
The attendees are explicitly listed after 'Present:'.
Action item owners are identified by the names preceding the tasks.
Due dates are extracted exactly as described or mapped to specific dates provided in the text.
"End of this week" for a Thursday meeting on March 12 is interpreted as the following Monday, March 13, based on the explicit date provided in parentheses.
known_risks: Interpretation of 'end of this week' could vary by organization policy (e.g., Friday vs Monday), but the text provides 'March 13' explicitly.

assumptions: The phrase 'end of this week' in the context of a Thursday meeting refers to the upcoming Monday or the end of the work week. Given the specific date provided in parentheses '(March 13)', that date is used as the due date.
known_risks: Ambiguity in 'end of this week' could potentially be interpreted as Friday March 13, but the text explicitly states March 13.
